mode: decay-only QAT (124-step decay from iter36 WS-final)
comment: First QAT proof: pure quant cost on the QAT'd model = +0.000018 imm / -0.000127 ahead (PTQ card int4+note int4 was +0.003569 imm; int2 worse) -> QAT dissolves the quant penalty in BOTH modes. Cost = the decay-only fine-tune raised fp32 by +0.002456 imm / +0.001129 ahead. NEXT: full WS+QAT (iter40) to recover that.

mode: full WS+QAT FROM SCRATCH (558 WS + 124 decay) -- REJECTED
comment: NEGATIVE RESULT: full WS+QAT from RANDOM INIT converges to a much worse minimum than decay-only QAT from the champion (deploy imm 0.310306 vs iter39 0.298538; +0.0118 worse). Quant-from-step-0 under high LR destabilizes, AND the quant cost itself jumped to +0.0045 (vs ~0 for iter39). LESSON: QAT must WARM-START from a good fp32 checkpoint, never from scratch. iter39 remains the QAT winner. Recover its +0.0025 via deck/preset growth or a longer WARM-STARTED QAT fine-tune.

mode: moderate grow [1,8,3,6,3] (deck 4->8, preset 3->6), fresh WS + decay-QAT
deploy_state: 0.27+1.59 KiB (card+note); +72,814 params in deck/preset (state-free on card)
comment: NEGATIVE for the grow hypothesis: moderate deck/preset grow deploys at imm 0.301564 = WORSE than iter39's 0.298538 (+0.0030). The quant cost is tiny (+0.000634 imm) -- QAT still dissolves the quant penalty -- but the fp32 ft-regress nearly DOUBLED (+0.004866 vs iter39 +0.002456). Cause: the grown arch must be FRESH WS-trained (cannot load champion weights into a different shape), and a fresh 18-epoch WS of a bigger model did NOT reach the polished champion's fp32 quality (undertraining / 100-user data may not reward extra capacity). So growing deck/preset did not buy back accuracy here; it cost it. iter39 (decay-QAT from the polished champion) REMAINS the QAT winner. Confound: fresh-train penalty is entangled with the grow effect; iter42 (more grow, also fresh) is the comparator.

mode: aggressive grow [1,16,3,12,3] (deck 4->16, preset 3->12), fresh WS + decay-QAT
deploy_state: 0.27+1.59 KiB (card+note); +218,442 params over champ in deck/preset
comment: DECISIVE NEGATIVE for the grow hypothesis: aggressive grow (411,242 params) deploys at imm 0.358399 -- FAILS the iter0 gate (>0.320975) and is far worse than both iter39 no-grow (0.298538) and iter41 moderate grow (0.301564). Per Andrews plan (do both, pick lower): lower-logloss grow is iter41, but BOTH lose to no-grow iter39 -> deck/preset GROW IS A DEAD END. Root cause: a grown arch must be FRESH WS-trained (cannot load champion weights into a different shape), and the bigger the model the more it undertrains in the 18-epoch/100-user WS budget -- so BOTH the fp32 fine-tune cost (+0.050846, ~10x iter39) AND the quant cost (+0.011489, vs ~0 for iter39) ballooned. iter39 stays QAT winner. Pivot: better/longer warm-started QAT + push note int2 (iter43), not grow.

mode: decay-only QAT (124-step decay from iter36 WS-final) -- NOTE INT2 target
deploy_state: 0.27+0.80 KiB (card+note); note int2 = the >=2x note target MET
comment: WIN: note int2 via QAT MEETS the >=2x note target (note int4 1.59 KiB -> note int2 0.80 KiB). Same proven recipe as iter39 (4-epoch decay from the iter36 champion) but card int2 + NOTE int2. Deploy imm 0.299469 PASSES the iter0 gate (<=0.320975, margin 0.0215); ahead 0.328570 PASSES. Cost vs champ fp32 = +0.003405 imm (vs iter39 card-int2+note-int4 +0.0025 imm): halving the note state costs only +0.000931 imm over iter39 -- excellent trade. The QAT fine-tune slightly IMPROVED fp32 (-0.000120 imm), so the entire +0.0034 is the int2+int2 quant penalty (not fine-tune regress). NEW DEPLOYED CHAMPION (replaces iter39): card int2 0.27 KiB + note int2 0.80 KiB, weights reference/rwkv_iter43_124.safetensors. Targets: note >=2x MET; card 0.15 KB still needs low-rank (int2 floors at 0.25 KiB).

mode: LONGER 8-epoch decay-QAT from iter36 WS-final -- card int2 + note int2
comment: BIG WIN -- improve QAT confirmed. Doubling the decay-QAT from 4 epochs (iter43) to 8 epochs drops deployed imm 0.299469 -> 0.295436 (-0.004033), and the deployed card-int2+note-int2 model now BEATS the fp32 champion in BOTH modes (imm -0.000627, ahead -0.003340 vs champ_fp32). Mechanism: longer QAT improved the BASE model fp32 by -0.003610 imm / -0.004712 ahead (it was undertrained at 4 epochs) AND kept the residual quant cost low (+0.002983 imm). Same state as iter43 (card 0.27 + note 0.80 KiB) but more accurate. NEW DEPLOYED CHAMPION (replaces iter43), weights reference/rwkv_iter44.safetensors (step 248). Lesson: more decay-QAT annealing helps; champion was undertrained. NEXT: iter45 = 16-epoch to find the plateau.

mode: 16-epoch decay-QAT from iter36 WS-final -- card int2 + note int2 (QAT plateau probe)
comment: QAT-epoch scaling near plateau. 16ep continues to drop deployed imm (8ep 0.295436 -> 16ep 0.292560, -0.002876) and beats fp32 champ by -0.003504 imm, so best-imm champion. BUT diminishing/mixed returns: deployed AHEAD crossed over (8ep 0.323291 -> 16ep 0.324638, +0.001347, still beats fp32 0.326631), and the int2 quant cost GREW (8ep +0.002983 -> 16ep +0.004742) as the harder-finetuned model becomes more int2-sensitive. Sequence imm: 4ep 0.299469 / 8ep 0.295436 / 16ep 0.292560 (gains shrinking: -0.0040 then -0.0029). STOP epoch-scaling at 16 (best imm@16ep, best ahead@8ep; both beat fp32). NEW DEPLOYED CHAMPION (best imm), weights reference/rwkv_iter45.safetensors (step 496); iter44 is ~tied (better ahead 0.323291). KEY FINDING: the fp32 BASE keeps improving strongly with more decay (qat_fp32 imm 0.296064 -> 0.292454 -> 0.287818 at 4/8/16ep) => the iter36 champion 2-epoch decay was UNDERTRAINED; a longer PLAIN (non-QAT) decay would likely improve the real eval100 benchmark, not just deploy. Revisit as a base-model improvement.

mode: iter45 weights + LOW-RANK card deploy (rank-2, int4 factors + int4 shifts) + note int2 -- PTQ, no retrain
config: card rank-2 int4 (lowrank) + note int2; shifts quantized (RWKV_QUANT_SHIFTS)
basis: 17-user gate (rust deploy vs rust fp32), HONEST shifts quantized
deploy_state: card 0.094 KiB (96 B: rank-2 int4 WKV 64 B + int4 shifts 32 B) + note 0.80 KiB
comment: BREAKTHROUGH -- BOTH hard targets MET. Low-rank rank-2 (int4 factors) for the card WKV matrix is SMALLER AND MORE ACCURATE than int2 full: card state 0.27 KiB int2 -> 0.094 KiB (96 B) rank-2 int4, AND deploy imm improves 0.295833 (honest int2 champ) -> 0.291471 (-0.004362). Beats the fp32 champion too (imm -0.004593, ahead -0.003028). Pure PTQ on iter45 QAT weights -- NO low-rank-specific training (QAT-for-lowrank could improve further). Mechanism: int2 coarsely quantizes all 1024 WKV floats (3 levels); rank-2 keeps the dominant 2 SVD components in int4 -> captures 98.7% energy far more faithfully (screen: analyze_card_rank.py). HONEST shift-quant note (RWKV_QUANT_SHIFTS=1): quantizing the 64 1-D token-shift floats cost the int2 champ +0.003273 imm (0.292560 shifts-fp32 -> 0.295833) but low-rank only +0.001065 (card shifts int4 not int2). Card target A: int2 floored at 0.27 KiB (256 B WKV); rank-2 int4 breaks it to 96 B. Engine: RWKV_STATE_LOWRANK_SCOPE=card:2:int4 + RWKV_QUANT_SHIFTS=1 (nalgebra SVD per-step = per-persist deploy model). NEW DEPLOYED CHAMPION config on iter45 weights.

mode: FIRST real low-rank QAT (card rank2-int4 lowrank QAT + note int2 QAT), 8-epoch decay -- REJECTED
comment: NEGATIVE: naive low-rank QAT (STE rank-2 truncation per step) deploys at imm 0.303617 -- WORSE than the low-rank PTQ on iter45 (0.291471, by +0.012) AND worse than champ_fp32 (+0.007553). The pure low-rank deploy cost on the QAT model BALLOONED to +0.010320 (vs ~+0.0037 for PTQ low-rank on iter45). MECHANISM: unlike int-quant (small element-wise error -> STE works), rank-2 truncation is a STRUCTURAL change; the identity STE gradient gives the model NO signal to concentrate energy in the top-2 singular directions, and the truncated-forward moving target drifts the model toward states that are HARDER to low-rank, not easier. LESSON: low-rank deploy should stay PTQ (PTQ low-rank already BEATS int2 and hits the 0.15 KB card target); naive STE QAT for low-rank is a DEAD END. A differentiable-SVD QAT (grad through truncation) could be tried but PTQ already suffices. The note int2 QAT part ran fine. DEPLOYED CHAMPION remains iter45 weights + card low-rank PTQ (0.291471) / both-low-rank PTQ (0.289137 preliminary).

mode: both-low-rank PTQ (card+note rank2-int4 + int4 shifts) on iter45 -- FULL 17-user gate incl 187 via fast Gram+eigen SVD
basis: champ_fp32 (iter36) imm 0.296064 / ahead 0.326631; qat_fp32 (iter45) imm 0.287818 / ahead 0.319089
deploy_state: card 96 B (rank-2 int4 WKV + int4 shifts) + note ~288 B (rank-2 int4)
comment: Re-confirmation with the FAST truncated SVD (Gram+symmetric-eigen replacing slow nalgebra full SVD; step 3): user 187 now 22s (was >35min hang) so the note-low-rank gate is practical and 187 is INCLUDED (prior 0.289137 was 16 users). 187 low-rank deploy is fine -- earlier hang/panic was pure SVD numerics, not divergence. Deployed champion.
